task.prompt: Write a function `choose_approver(request, approvers)` that selects an approver for a request.

gold_spec.goal: Return the id of the best eligible approver for a business request.

outputs.name: approver_id
outputs.description: selected approver id, or None when no approver is eligible

preconditions: request is a dictionary and approvers is a list of dictionaries.
postconditions: An approver is eligible only when active is exactly True and id is present.
An approver may not approve their own request; exclude approvers whose id equals request['requester_id'].
Exclude approvers whose conflict_user_ids contains the requester id; missing conflict_user_ids means no conflicts.
Treat missing limit_cents as 0. The approver limit must be greater than or equal to request['amount_cents'], with missing request amount treated as 0.
The request department must appear in approver['departments'] or the departments list must contain '*'. Missing departments matches nothing.
The request region must appear in approver['regions'] or the regions list must contain '*'. Missing regions matches nothing.
If request['category'] is 'security', the approver role must be 'security' or 'compliance'.
If request amount is at least 1000000 cents, the approver role must be 'finance_director'.
Among eligible approvers, choose the one with the lowest priority value, treating missing priority as 100.
Break priority ties by lowest last_assigned_at, treating missing last_assigned_at as 0.
Break any remaining tie by lexicographically smallest approver id.
Return None if no approver is eligible.
edge_cases: Wildcard departments and regions match any request value.
Security requests require security/compliance role even when a manager has enough approval limit.
High-value requests require finance_director role even when another approver has enough approval limit.
acceptance_criteria: Selection is deterministic for every input order.
The function returns only an eligible approver id or None.

known_weakness.description: The raw prompt makes it easy to choose the first approver with enough limit, missing conflict checks, wildcard matching, special-category role constraints, and deterministic tie-breaking.
known_weakness.naive_spec_gap: models often implement partial eligibility and unstable first-match selection instead of full policy filtering followed by explicit tie-breaking
